news-global-econ.title: US destroyer interdicts two oil tankers trying to leave Iran during Trump's blockade
news-global-econ.description: A U.S. destroyer interdicted two oil tankers trying to leave Iran as part of the Trump administration's ongoing blockade on Iranian ports, a report said.

news-global-econ.title: The Iranian regime was built on ‘vicious antisemitism’ following the 1979 Islamic Revolution
news-global-econ.description: For over 2,500 years, Jews lived in Persia. After the 1979 Islamic Revolution, their community was decimated by persecution and forced emigration.

news-global-econ.title: Your favorite fast food app is playing mind games and charging you for the privilege
news-global-econ.description: Your $8 fast food order may balloon to $22 thanks to fee stacking, including delivery fees, service fees and the sneaky new small order fee penalty.

news-global-econ.title: Where you live could shape your risk of cancer mortality, study suggests
news-global-econ.description: New research reveals a widening gap in cancer mortality improvement between high-income urban areas and low-income rural U.S. counties.

news-global-econ.title: Codelco in talks with Hindustan Copper for Chile copper joint venture
news-global-econ.description: Indian companies, meanwhile, are looking to Chile, which boasts the world’s biggest copper reserves, to secure supply, integrate upstream and stay competitive in a tightening global market

news-global-soc.title: Stop calling this brinkmanship. Trump's Hormuz move is the real pressure
news-global-soc.description: President Trump's naval blockade cutting off Iran's oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz is the only play left after failed Islamabad negotiations.

news-global-soc.title: Memory chip crunch and Iran war lead phone market decline, IDC says
news-global-soc.description: Global smartphone market declines 4.1% amid memory chip shortages and Iran war, with only Apple and Samsung showing growth.

news-global-soc.title: Trump: Iran Conflict ‘Very Close to Being Over’ -- Tehran ‘Wants to Make a Deal Very Badly,’ Talks Could Resume Within Days
news-global-soc.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said Wednesdaythe war with Iran is “very close to being over,” arguing recent U.S. actions prevented Tehran from obtaining a nuclear weapon while signaling negotiations could resume imminently as the regime “want[s] to make a deal …
